article_title: Four Private Spacecraft Line Up for Lunar Landings in 2026 as NASA's Commercial Moon Program Expands

content_preview.title: Four Private Spacecraft Line Up for Lunar Landings in 2026 as NASA's Commercial Moon Program Expands
content_preview.summary: Astrobotic, Intuitive Machines, Firefly Aerospace, and Blue Origin are each preparing lunar lander missions for 2026 under NASA's CLPS program, marking an unprecedented year for commercial deep-space exploration.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

At least four commercially built lunar landers are targeting Moon missions in 2026, making it the busiest year yet for NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) initiative. Astrobotic's Griffin-1, Intuitive Machines' IM-3, Firefly Aerospace's Blue Ghost Mission 2, and Blue Origin's Blue Moon Mark 1 are all in various stages of preparation, collectively carrying rovers, scientific instruments, and technology demonstrations to the lunar surface.
